questions.question: The fundamental attribution error refers to the tendency to:

options.text: Overestimate dispositional factors and underestimate situational factors when explaining others' behavior
Overestimate situational factors and underestimate dispositional factors when explaining others' behavior
Attribute our own successes to effort and our failures to bad luck
Assume that all members of a group share the same personality traits

questions.explanation: The fundamental attribution error is the tendency to overestimate the role of internal, dispositional factors (personality) and underestimate the role of external, situational factors when explaining why other people behave as they do.

questions.question: Social psychology is best defined as the scientific study of how:

options.text: Mental disorders develop and can be treated
Individuals' thoughts, feelings, and behaviors are influenced by the presence of others
Groups evolve and maintain their organizational structures
Personality traits remain stable across the lifespan

questions.explanation: Social psychology specifically examines how the actual, imagined, or implied presence of others influences individual thoughts, feelings, and behaviors, distinguishing it from other branches of psychology.
